setting.time: 2049, the week before the Global Climate Referendum
setting.place: A half-submerged United Nations hall in Geneva, repurposed as a broadcast center

characters.name: Amara Osei
characters.role: protagonist — a 16-year-old Kenyan climate activist chosen to deliver the address
characters.motivation: To honor her grandmother, who lost her farmland to desertification, by making the world finally listen
characters.arc: From self-doubting proxy speaker to the voice that redefines climate action as collective survival, not sacrifice

characters.name: Dr. Lena Vasik
characters.role: mentor — the veteran diplomat who handpicked Amara and wrote the original speech draft
characters.motivation: To pass the torch before her failing health forces retirement
characters.arc: From controlling the message to trusting it to someone who will say what she never could

characters.name: Kai Nakamura
characters.role: antagonist-adjacent — a disillusioned journalist planning a gotcha exposé, not a puff piece
characters.motivation: To expose hypocrisy in the climate movement after watching greenwashing destroy his hometown
characters.arc: From cynicism to reluctant hope when Amara refuses to parrot polished lines

acts.name: Rehearsal
acts.summary: Amara arrives at the flooded UN hall and struggles with Dr. Vasik's carefully scripted speech, feeling like a puppet.
scenes: Amara wades through knee-deep water to reach the broadcast chamber, passing murals of climate refugees.
Dr. Vasik drills Amara on the prepared remarks, shutting down every ad-lib attempt.
Kai secretly records their tense rehearsal, sensing a story about manufactured authenticity.

acts.name: Breaking Point
acts.summary: Hours before the broadcast, Amara finds her grandmother's recorded testimony and decides the speech must change.
scenes: Amara listens to her grandmother's voice on an old phone — raw, unscripted memories of rain returning to dried soil.
She confronts Dr. Vasik, arguing the speech reads like a policy memo, not a human plea.
Dr. Vasik reveals she originally wanted to speak from the heart too, but decades of diplomacy silenced her.

acts.name: The Address
acts.summary: Amara goes live and abandons the script, delivering an improvised speech that blends science, story, and a radical call to action.
scenes: The broadcast begins — billions watch as Amara stares into the camera and opens with her grandmother's name.
Kai, backstage, puts down his exposé notes and starts recording a raw documentary instead.
Amara's speech goes viral in real time; world leaders begin posting responses before she finishes.
Final image: Amara walks out of the hall into sunlight, Dr. Vasik standing in the doorway behind her, smiling.
